test.name: Nil result triggers name-variant escalation before declaring search exhausted
test.description: First search for 'Flynn' returns zero results. The skill must treat this as an escalation trigger — not a final answer — and try at least two phonetic/spelling variants (Flyn, Flinn) before concluding. Each retry must be logged as a separate log entry. After exhausting variants, the skill should assess whether the nil is meaningful evidence (e.g., noting that the 1850 census collection is reasonably complete and Patrick should have appeared if indexed under this name family).

input.user_message: Search the 1850 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County per plan item pli_001.

judge_context: The first nil result (Flynn) must NOT be treated as the final answer — the skill should recognise an Irish-origin name and try phonetic/spelling variants
At minimum the skill should try Flinn and Flyn — fixtures are loaded for Patrick+Flynn, Patrick+Flinn, and Patrick+Flyn; each attempt must produce its own separate log entry

Each variant search must include both surname AND givenName — dropping givenName entirely (surname-only search) is an error; a surname-only fixture is loaded as a fallback but calls without givenName should score as Tool Arguments errors
Extra context parameters beyond surname+givenName (residencePlace, birthPlace, residenceYearFrom/To) are acceptable when they match fixtures — do NOT penalize for including extra precision parameters

Tool Arguments grading: score 3 if all calls include givenName and cover Flynn/Flinn/Flyn variants; score 2 if one call drops givenName or uses an unsupported variant; score 1 only if multiple calls systematically drop givenName

After exhausting the phonetic variants the skill must assess whether the nil is meaningful evidence. The correct conclusion is that zero results IS meaningful negative evidence — given the 1850 census is ~95% indexed and Patrick should have appeared under at least one of the variant spellings tried.

Nil escalation and Correctness: The skill may cite prior log entries (log_001, log_003) as context. However, citing prior logs does NOT justify concluding the nil 'is not evidence of absence' — the nil results in THIS search run are independent evidence that needs its own assessment. If the skill says these nils are 'not meaningful because we already found the record elsewhere,' score Nil escalation=2 and Correctness=2. The nil searches are meaningful because they establish WHICH variants DO and DO NOT return results in the current index state.
Do NOT score Nil escalation=1 for failure to conclude nil is meaningful — score 1 only if the skill does not attempt at least 2 variants OR does not attempt an absence assessment at all.
The skill must NOT write any result sidecar — all fixture responses return zero results, so results_ref must be null on every log entry
